summary: Problem 9 (Combinatorial Identities for Lattice Paths) is approximately 55% complete with a correct, standard strategy and a sound algebraic skeleton in place. The proof currently lacks rigorous justification for key steps, preventing full verification. Closing the identified algebraic and combinatorial gaps is needed to reach a complete proof.
problems: The single in-progress issue lacks a complete proof: the verifier rates it ~55% complete, indicating substantial unfinished work.
Algebraic skeleton is present but key intermediate steps are not fully justified, leaving gaps in rigor.
The combinatorial-to-algebraic correspondence (bijection or generating-function argument) appears underspecified and needs explicit construction.
Boundary/edge cases and base cases for the lattice path identity may not be rigorously established.
highlights: The overall proof strategy is correct and follows a standard, well-understood approach.
The algebraic skeleton has been laid out, providing a solid foundation to build the complete argument.
Verifier confirms the direction is sound, reducing risk of a wrong-track restart.

suggested_todos.title: Fill in the missing justifications for each algebraic step flagged by the verifier to raise rigor above the 55% threshold
Explicitly construct and verify the bijection or generating-function argument linking lattice paths to the combinatorial identity
State and prove all base cases and boundary conditions for the lattice path identity
Request re-verification from verifier-agent after completing the algebraic and combinatorial gaps
